components.name: monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.evidence: P. aeruginosa was cultured in minimal media (22 mM KH2PO4, 42 mM Na2HPO4, 8.6 mM NaCl, 2.0 mM MgSO4, 5.0 μM FeSO4, pH 7.0)

components.evidence: minimal media (22 mM KH2PO4, 42 mM Na2HPO4, 8.6 mM NaCl, 2.0 mM MgSO4, 5.0 μM FeSO4, pH 7.0)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: grown in 5 mL minimal media supplemented with 20 mM D-glucose and 10 mM NH4Cl

components.name: ammonium chloride (NH4Cl)
components.evidence: grown in 5 mL minimal media supplemented with 20 mM D-glucose and 10 mM NH4Cl

components.name: oxygen (O2)
components.evidence: grown for 24 h (230 rpm, 37 °C) ... incubated in either 15 mL (in 150-mL flasks, 230 rpm, 37 °C) or 200 μL (in 96-well plates, continuous shaking 282 CPM (3 mm), 37 °C)

components.name: water (H2O)
components.evidence: grown in 5 mL minimal media (implicit aqueous solvent)

media.base_medium: Lundgren et al. 2021 defined minimal medium for P. aeruginosa PAO1 (phosphate-buffered salts + FeSO4)

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 74 (Lundgren BR et al., BMC Microbiol 21:83, 2021; PMC7962211)
sources_chased.note: Lundgren 2021 states the identical recipe (22 mM KH2PO4, 42 mM Na2HPO4, 8.6 mM NaCl, 2.0 mM MgSO4, 5.0 μM FeSO4, pH 7.0) with NO added trace-element solution and NO vitamins, and cites no earlier source for it. Base medium confirmed complete; nothing new to add.

media.notes: This is a fully defined minimal medium with only five salts plus an as-indicated C/N supplement (20 mM D-glucose + 10 mM NH4Cl for the standard condition; the paper also uses 10 mM β-Asp-Arg, 10 mM L-aspartate, or 10 mM L-arginine). Chasing the cited base reference (Lundgren 2021) confirmed there is deliberately NO trace-element mix (only 5 µM FeSO4) and NO vitamins. The Ca2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Mn2+, Zn2+ and Fe3+ entries in the target's current_components are NOT part of the defined recipe (they have no textual source in either the paper or the base reference; trace metals are present only as unquantified salt impurities) and were dropped to avoid fabrication. Ionic decomposition of the real salts: K+ 22 mM; Na+ ~92.6 mM (84 from Na2HPO4 + 8.6 from NaCl); phosphate ~64 mM; Cl- ~18.6 mM (8.6 NaCl + 10 NH4Cl); SO4 ~2.005 mM; Mg2+ 2.0 mM; Fe2+ 0.005 mM; NH4+ 10 mM.
